revision.text:
[[ja:謁播神社]] {{Expand Japanese|謁播神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Atsuwa Shrine | native_name = 謁播神社 | image = Okazaki-Atsuwa-Jinja-2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Atsuwa Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.991141|137.177248}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Atsuwa Shrine'''|謁播神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mikawa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * Province|ja|三河国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} ** Series ordinal: 9 ** P155: {{ill|Q22117916|ltq=Q22117916|qid=Q22117916|1=Inasaki Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039572|ltq=Q135039572|qid=Q135039572|1=Washitorino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Okazaki, Aichi|lt=Okazaki|ar|أوكازاكي (آيتشي)|azb|اوکازاکی، آیچی|az|Okazaki|be|Акадзакі|bg|Окадзаки|ca|Okazaki|ceb|Okazaki (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Окадзаки|ckb|ئۆکازاکی، ئایچی|cs|Okazaki (Aiči)|de|Okazaki|en|Okazaki, Aichi|envoyage|Okazaki|eo|Okazaki|es|Okazaki (Aichi)|eu|Okazaki (Aichi)|fa|اوکازاکی|fi|Okazaki|fr|Okazaki|ga|Okazaki|gl|Okazaki|hr|Okazaki|hy|Օկաձակի|id|Okazaki|it|Okazaki (Aichi)|ja|岡崎市|javoyage|岡崎市|kk|Окадзаки|ko|오카자키시|lt|Okadzakis|mdf|Окадзаки|mg|Okazaki|ms|Okazaki, Aichi|mzn|اوکازاکی، آیچی|nl|Okazaki|no|Okazaki|pl|Okazaki|pt|Okazaki|ro|Okazaki|ru|Окадзаки|sh|Okazaki|simple|Okazaki, Aichi|sr|Оказаки|sv|Okazaki|tg|Оказаки|th|โอกาซากิ|tl|Okazaki|tr|Okazaki|tt|Окадзаки|uk|Окадзакі (Айті)|ur|اوکازاکی، ایچی|uz|Okazaki (Aichi)|vi|Okazaki, Aichi|war|Okazaki, Aichi|zh_classical|岡崎市|zh_min_nan|Okazaki-chhī|zh_yue|岡崎|zh|岡崎市|qid=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}} * {{ill|Q768158|ltq=Q768158|qid=Q768158|1=Mikawa Province}} * {{ill|Nukata District|lt=Nukata district|ceb|Nukata-gun|de|Nukata-gun|en|Nukata District|es|Distrito de Nukata (Aichi)|fa|شهرستان نوکاتا، آیچی|fr|District de Nukata|id|Distrik Nukata, Aichi|it|Distretto di Nukata|ja|額田郡|ko|누카타군|pl|Powiat Nukata|ru|Нуката (уезд)|sr|Област Нуката|tl|Distrito ng Nukata, Aichi|uk|Повіт Нуката|zh|額田郡|qid=Q787290|1=Nukata District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.991141|137.177248}} == image (P18) == * Okazaki-Atsuwa-Jinja-2.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181470 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7180305000984 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県岡崎市東阿知和町字北山39番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:謁播神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28690200}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Atsuwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Okazaki-Atsuwa-Jinja-2.jpg|250px|Atsuwa Shrine]]
Shrine name marker and worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki City|Q242783|qid=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Higashi-Achiwa-chō|Q17223219|qid=Q17223219|1=Higashi-achiwa-chō, Okazaki}} Aza Kitayama 39 |Latitude degrees = 34|Latitude minutes = 59|Latitude seconds = 27.6 |Longitude degrees = 137|Longitude minutes = 10|Longitude seconds = 37.6 |Enshrined deities = Chihaya-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Annual festival = |Map = Japan Aichi }} '''Atsuwa Shrine''' (あつわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Higashi-Achiwa-chō|Q17223219|qid=Q17223219|1=Higashi-achiwa-chō, Okazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki City|Q242783|qid=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. === Overview === It is one of the two shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Nukata District|Q787290|qid=Q787290|1=Nukata District}} listed in the *{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}*, {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=vols. 9–10|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The enshrined deities are Chihaya-no-Mikoto and {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}. The exact date of founding is unclear, but the *Montoku Jitsuroku* from the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Montoku|Q357225|qid=Q357225|1=Emperor Montoku}} (850–858) records that “in the first year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} (851), Atsuwa Shrine was granted the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.” The *Mikawa no Kuni Shinmyō Meichō*, said to have been compiled from the late Heian to the Kamakura period, records it as “Atsuiwa Daimyōjin of Senior Third Rank”{{Sfn|‘Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties’|pp=223-224}}. In volume 10 (Kunizukuri Honki) of the *Sendai Kuji Hongi*, compiled in the early Heian period, it is stated that Chihaya-no-Mikoto—a fifth-generation descendant of Izumo Shikishō, an ancestor of the Mononobe clan—was appointed as the first {{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q2483673|qid=Q2483673|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}} of Mikawa Province. Atsuwa Shrine is therefore thought to have been a shrine worshipped by the Mononobe clan lineage, part of the former provincial governors who held great power in western Mikawa{{Sfn|‘Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties’|pp=223-224}}. In the early modern period it was called Kasuga Shrine, and records state that it was administered by the bettō temple Shōren-ji; this is written in the local gazetteer *Mikawa no Kuni Futaba-matsu*, compiled around the fifth year of {{ill|lt=Genbun|Q1145754|qid=Q1145754|1=Genbun}} (1740). Until the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, shrine rites were conducted by shrine monks from the adjacent Shōrin-ji temple. In the fifth year of Meiji (1872), it was designated a gōsha (village shrine). Within the precincts stands a gate serving as the shrine gate, said to have been relocated from the “Nenbutsudō Akamon” of {{ill|lt=Okazaki Castle|Q1013445|qid=Q1013445|1=Okazaki Castle}}{{Cite web|Japanese|url=https://okazakikeikan.boo-log.com/e117694.html | title=Atsuwa Shrine | work=Okazaki Iitoko Scenic Blog | publisher={{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|qid=Q11472848|lt=Okazaki City|1=Okazaki City Hall}} Urban Design Division | date=2011-04-04 | accessdate=2021-07-20 }}. === Festivals === In {{ill|lt=Higashi-Achiwa-chō|Q17223219|qid=Q17223219|1=Higashi-achiwa-chō, Okazaki}}, a gagaku musicians’ association formed by volunteer parishioners has been handed down since the Taishō period. At Atsuwa Shrine’s three annual festivals (the Kinen-sai in February, the main festival in October, and the {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} on November 23), miko dances and gagaku performances by the local musicians’ association are offered{{Cite web|Japanese|url= https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/29iwadu.pdf | title= Okazaki Machi Monogatari 29: Iwazu School District | publisher= Okazaki City, 100th Anniversary of Municipal Incorporation Website | format=PDF | date=2017-1 | accessdate=2020-07-26 }}. === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} ** Take the bus bound for Okuden Jinya, get off at “Aoki-chō” {{ill|lt=bus stop|Q953806|qid=Q953806|lt=bus stop|1=Bus stop}}, then walk approximately 1,500 meters east. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition: History of Okazaki City, General Volume 20 |publisher = Editorial Committee for the New History of Okazaki City |page = 16 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|Japanese |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = Okazaki City Hall |ref = {{SfnRef|‘Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties’}} }} === Related articles === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|qid=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Atsuwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Atsuwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Atsuwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]]
